MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS IMPERFECT FOODS, INC., BENJAMIN SIMON, and BENJAMIN CHESLER's Amended MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION:

Defendant Imperfect Foods, Inc.'s amended notice of motion and motion for summary adjudication is ordered off calendar as untimely. California Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(a)(2) requires that motions for summary judgment provide no less than 75 days' notice. Here, Defendant filed the Motion for Summary Adjudication on December 3, 2021, noticing the hearing date for February 16, 2022. Defendants then served the Amended Notice of Motion and Motion for Summary Adjudication on January 6, 2022, continuing the hearing date by two days to February 18, 2022. The Amended Notice effectively provided only 43 days' notice of the hearing. There does not appear to have been any stipulation between the parties to shorten time, and the court has no authority to, for example, continue the hearing date to cure this defect. (See Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1268; Urshan v. Musicians' Credit Union (2004) 102 Cal.App.4th 758.)

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/SRB)

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF RONALD CLARK's Motion For Preliminary Injunction.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion for preliminary injunction is denied. Plaintiff filed this action on May 13, 2019, and could have sought injunctive relief at that time.
